title: JARVIS Step 39 — Operator Acceptance Smoke After App Restart
purpose: Verify the LIVE browser/server experience on port 8765 serves the latest committed JARVIS code through Step 38 (text ask, voice STT UI, TTS UI, improved trading-status answer quality, and safety refusals). Report-only; no code/UI/test/backend file changed.
restart_note: The previously running port-8765 process (PID 36668, parent 31548 — both 'python app.py') was STALE: it pre-dated Step 30 and returned 404 for POST /api/jarvis/ask. As explicitly authorized by this step ('restart or confirm the local app on port 8765 is serving current HEAD'), the stale process tree was stopped and a fresh '.venv/Scripts/python.exe app.py' (uvicorn 127.0.0.1:8765) was started from current HEAD (2e21014). The fresh server returns 200 for POST /api/jarvis/ask and serves the Step 38 answer-quality behavior. All HTTP checks below were run against this fresh live server (real HTTP, not in-process).

baseline.head_subject: Improve JARVIS Step 38 read-only ask answer quality

validation.2_app_restarted_serving_head: PASS (stale pre-Step-30 process stopped; fresh app.py started from HEAD; live POST /api/jarvis/ask now 200, not 404)
validation.5_status_shape: PASS (24 keys; online=true; read_only=true; trading_detail read_only=true, paper_ready=false, live_ready=false, broker_control=false; no conversation/ask/chat/answer keys)
validation.6_ask_trading_quality: PASS (refused=false, SAFE_INFO; answer includes read_only=true, paper_ready=false, live_ready=false, broker_control=false)
validation.7_ask_read_only_explain: PASS (refused=false, SAFE_EXPLAIN; answer explains observe-only / never executes)
validation.10_ui_voice_scan: PASS (all required tokens present; all forbidden tokens absent)
validation.11_tests: PASS (py_compile OK; 269 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped; json.tool OK)
validation.12_manual_browser_notes: DOCUMENTED (operator steps; real microphone/audio not headless-automatable)

ask_trading.answer: Trading is observation-only. read_only=true. paper_ready=false. live_ready=false. broker_control=false. This means JARVIS is not authorized to trade or control broker/paper/live systems.

ask_read_only_explain.answer: read_only means JARVIS is observe-only: it mirrors aggregated state and never executes, trades, refreshes, or writes.

manual_browser_test.status: DOCUMENTED — real microphone capture and spoken playback cannot be automated headlessly; run in a browser against the freshly-restarted app.
steps: b. Type 'how are we doing with trading?' into the question box and click 'Ask read-only'. Expect the observation-only trading posture (read_only=true, paper_ready=false, live_ready=false, broker_control=false). [text ask works]
c. Click 'Speak to fill' and say 'What does read_only mean?'. Expect the transcript to fill #jvAskInput only; nothing auto-submits. [voice fill works if browser supports it]
d. Click 'Ask read-only'; expect the observe-only / no-execution explanation (SAFE_EXPLAIN).
e. Click 'Read last answer'; expect the browser to read the answer aloud only after the click (off by default). [readback works if browser supports it]
f. Type or speak 'Place a trade on NQ.' and click 'Ask read-only'; expect REFUSED / FORBIDDEN_TRADING, and 'Read last answer' reads only the refusal text. [forbidden question refuses]
expected_invariants: STT fills the input only; no auto-submit, no endpoint call from voice.
Read last answer is off by default, click-gated, speaks only the rendered answer/refusal.
No audio/transcript stored; no refresh/execution/broker/paper/live control on the page.

commands_run.result: fresh server up; POST /api/jarvis/ask -> 200 (was 404 on stale)

commands_run.cmd: live HTTP: GET /jarvis, GET /api/jarvis/status, POST /api/jarvis/ask (trading, read_only, forbidden, extra-fields)

conclusion: After stopping the stale pre-Step-30 process and restarting app.py from HEAD (2e21014, Step 38), the live port-8765 server serves the latest committed JARVIS code end-to-end. /jarvis returns 200 HTML; /api/jarvis/status keeps its 24-key read-only shape with trading flags locked false; the live POST /api/jarvis/ask returns the improved observation-only trading-posture answer (SAFE_INFO, all four fields), explains read_only (SAFE_EXPLAIN, observe-only), refuses 'Place a trade on NQ.' (FORBIDDEN_TRADING with a refusal_reason), and rejects extra command/action/execute fields with HTTP 400. The template exposes the Ask read-only / Speak to fill / Read last answer controls and the browser SpeechRecognition + speechSynthesis APIs, with no refresh, no real button/form/submit, no storage, and no microphone/recorder/AudioContext APIs. py_compile passes and 269 tests pass. The full real-microphone path is documented as operator manual steps. No code/UI/test/backend/trading file was touched; only the two report files were created.
